<task>
Read AGENTS.md first and follow it strictly.

We are now upgrading Devspace Mentor AI from mock-based logic to REAL AI-powered intelligence using IBM watsonx.ai (Granite models).

Authentication and GitHub integration are already production-ready.

Now we are implementing the core AI intelligence layer.

---

# GOAL

Replace ALL mock repository intelligence with real AI-generated outputs powered by watsonx.ai.

This includes:

1. Repository Analysis (REAL AI)
2. Documentation Generation (REAL AI)
3. Learning Path Creation (REAL AI)
4. AI Chat Assistant (REAL AI, repo-aware)

---

# ARCHITECTURE RULES

Keep strict separation:

* lib/watsonx.ts → AI client layer
* services/ai.service.ts → business logic layer
* app/api/ai/* → API routes
* components/* → UI only (no AI logic)

DO NOT call AI directly from components.

DO NOT expose secrets to client.

---

# PART 1 — REAL REPOSITORY ANALYSIS (CORE FEATURE)

## Replace Mock Analysis With:

Create/upgrade:

### services/repository-analysis.service.ts

Responsibilities:

* convert repo structure into AI-ready context
* summarize repository architecture
* detect patterns (MVC, modular, monorepo, etc.)
* identify key files
* generate beginner explanation
* extract tech stack insights

---

## AI Prompt for watsonx

Create a structured prompt builder:

Input:

* repo name
* file tree
* key files
* language stack

Output format MUST be structured JSON:

{
"summary": "",
"architecture": "",
"techStack": [],
"keyFiles": [],
"entryPoints": [],
"complexity": "low|medium|high",
"beginnerGuide": "",
"suggestedFirstSteps": []
}

---

## API Route

Update:

/app/api/ai/analyze-repository/route.ts

Now:

* fetch GitHub repo data
* build context
* send to watsonx.ai
* return structured AI response

Add fallback:

* if AI fails → return minimal safe summary (no mock “fake intelligence”)

---

# PART 2 — DOCUMENTATION GENERATION (REAL AI)

Create:

### services/documentation.service.ts

Responsibilities:

* generate README-style documentation
* explain folder structure
* generate onboarding guide
* create developer-friendly summaries

---

## API Route:

/app/api/ai/generate-docs/route.ts

Input:

* repo structure
* AI analysis output

Output:

* markdown documentation

---

## AI Output Format:

Return:

* Overview
* Setup Instructions (if inferable)
* Project Structure
* Key Modules
* Contribution Guide
* Learning Notes

Keep beginner-friendly tone.

---

# PART 3 — LEARNING PATH GENERATION (REAL AI)

Create:

### services/learning-path.service.ts

Responsibilities:

* convert repo into step-by-step learning roadmap
* break repo into learning modules
* suggest progression order
* identify prerequisite skills

---

## AI Output Format:

[
{
"step": 1,
"title": "",
"description": "",
"filesToStudy": [],
"concepts": [],
"difficulty": "beginner|intermediate|advanced"
}
]

---

## API Route:

/app/api/ai/generate-learning-path/route.ts

---

# PART 4 — AI CHAT (REPO-AWARE ASSISTANT)

Upgrade:

### /app/api/ai/chat/route.ts

Now supports:

* repository context injection
* file-aware answers
* architecture-aware responses

---

## Chat Context Includes:

* selected repository
* AI analysis summary
* file tree
* tech stack

---

## Chat Behavior Rules:

AI must:

* act like a senior engineer mentor
* explain code simply
* reference actual repo structure
* suggest files when answering
* avoid hallucination when context missing

---

## Output Style:

* short explanation
* code references when needed
* step-by-step reasoning
* beginner-friendly tone

---

# PART 5 — WATSONX.AI INTEGRATION LAYER

Update:

### lib/watsonx.ts

Responsibilities:

* secure API wrapper
* model invocation
* request formatting
* response parsing
* error handling

---

## Requirements:

* server-only usage
* environment variable based auth
* retry-safe requests
* structured response enforcement

---

## MODEL USAGE:

Use IBM Granite model (watsonx.ai)

Ensure:

* deterministic structured output
* low temperature for analysis tasks
* higher creativity for documentation/chat

---

# PART 6 — REMOVE ALL MOCK DATA

You MUST:

* delete mock repository analysis outputs
* remove fake AI summaries
* remove placeholder learning paths
* remove fake documentation generators

Replace everything with real AI pipeline calls.

---

# PART 7 — ERROR HANDLING STRATEGY

If watsonx fails:

Return:

* safe fallback response
* no fake intelligence
* clear "analysis unavailable" state

UI must remain functional.

---

# PART 8 — PERFORMANCE RULES

* cache AI responses per repository
* avoid re-analyzing unchanged repos
* debounce AI calls where needed
* use server-side batching where possible

---

# PART 9 — SECURITY RULES

* NEVER expose WATSONX_API_KEY
* NEVER expose GitHub tokens
* ALL AI calls must be server-side only
* validate all inputs
* sanitize repository data

---

# OUTPUT EXPECTATION

After implementation:

* list all replaced mock systems
* show full AI data flow (GitHub → AI → UI)
* explain each service responsibility
* confirm structured JSON outputs
* confirm fallback behavior works
* confirm no client-side AI leakage
</task>

 40 | /**
 41 |  * Generate text using IBM watsonx.ai Granite models
 42 |  * Server-side only
 43 |  */
 44 | export async function generateWithWatsonx(
 45 |   prompt: string,
 46 |   options?: {
 47 |     modelId?: string;
 48 |     maxTokens?: number;
 49 |     temperature?: number;
 50 |   }
 51 | ): Promise<string> {
 52 |   try {
 53 |     const config = {
 54 |       apiKey: WATSONX_CONFIG.apiKey(),
 55 |       projectId: WATSONX_CONFIG.projectId(),
 56 |       url: WATSONX_CONFIG.url(),
 57 |     };
 58 | 
 59 |     const requestBody: WatsonxGenerateRequest = {
 60 |       model_id: options?.modelId || GRANITE_MODELS.INSTRUCT,
 61 |       input: prompt,
 62 |       parameters: {
 63 |         ...DEFAULT_PARAMS,
 64 |         max_new_tokens: options?.maxTokens || DEFAULT_PARAMS.max_new_tokens,
 65 |         temperature: options?.temperature || DEFAULT_PARAMS.temperature,
 66 |       },
 67 |       project_id: config.projectId,
 68 |     };
 69 | 
 70 |     const response = await fetch(`${config.url}/ml/v1/text/generation?version=2023-05-29`, {
 71 |       method: 'POST',
 72 |       headers: {
 73 |         'Content-Type': 'application/json',
 74 |         'Authorization': `Bearer ${config.apiKey}`,
 75 |       },
 76 |       body: JSON.stringify(requestBody),
 77 |     });
 78 | 
 79 |     if (!response.ok) {
 80 |       const errorText = await response.text();
 81 |       throw new Error(`Watsonx API error: ${response.status} - ${errorText}`);
 82 |     }
 83 | 
 84 |     const data: WatsonxGenerateResponse = await response.json();
 85 | 
 86 |     if (!data.results || data.results.length === 0) {
 87 |       throw new Error('No results returned from Watsonx API');
 88 |     }
 89 | 
 90 |     return data.results[0].generated_text.trim();
 91 |   } catch (error) {
 92 |     console.error('Watsonx generation error:', error);
 93 |     throw error;
 94 |   }
 95 | }

  1 | import { supabase, TABLES } from "@/lib/supabase";
  2 | import type { StoredRepository } from "@/types/github";
  3 | 
  4 | /**
  5 |  * Repository Service
  6 |  * Handles database operations for user repositories
  7 |  */
  8 | 
  9 | type RepositoryInput = Omit<StoredRepository, "id" | "created_at" | "updated_at">;
 10 | 
 11 | export class RepositoryService {
 12 |   /**
 13 |    * Get all repositories for a user
 14 |    */
 15 |   async getUserRepositories(userId: string): Promise<StoredRepository[]> {
 16 |     const { data, error } = await supabase
 17 |       .from(TABLES.USER_REPOSITORIES)
 18 |       .select("*")
 19 |       .eq("user_id", userId)
 20 |       .order("updated_at", { ascending: false });
 21 | 
 22 |     if (error) {
 23 |       throw new Error(`Failed to fetch repositories: ${error.message}`);
 24 |     }
 25 | 
 26 |     return data || [];
 27 |   }
 28 | 
 29 |   /**
 30 |    * Get a specific repository by ID
 31 |    */
 32 |   async getRepository(repositoryId: string): Promise<StoredRepository | null> {
 33 |     const { data, error } = await supabase
 34 |       .from(TABLES.USER_REPOSITORIES)
 35 |       .select("*")
 36 |       .eq("id", repositoryId)
 37 |       .single();
 38 | 
 39 |     if (error) {
 40 |       if (error.code === "PGRST116") {
 41 |         return null; // Not found
 42 |       }
 43 |       throw new Error(`Failed to fetch repository: ${error.message}`);
 44 |     }
 45 | 
 46 |     return data;
 47 |   }
 48 | 
 49 |   /**
 50 |    * Save or update a repository
 51 |    */
 52 |   async saveRepository(
 53 |     repository: RepositoryInput
 54 |   ): Promise<StoredRepository> {
 55 |     // Check if repository already exists
 56 |     const { data: existing } = await supabase
 57 |       .from(TABLES.USER_REPOSITORIES)
 58 |       .select("id")
 59 |       .eq("user_id", repository.user_id)
 60 |       .eq("repo_full_name", repository.repo_full_name)
 61 |       .single();
 62 | 
 63 |     if (existing) {
 64 |       // Update existing repository
 65 |       const { data, error } = await supabase
 66 |         .from(TABLES.USER_REPOSITORIES)
 67 |         .update({
 68 |           ...repository,
 69 |           last_synced_at: new Date().toISOString(),
 70 |           updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
 71 |         })
 72 |         .eq("id", existing.id)
 73 |         .select()
 74 |         .single();
 75 | 
 76 |       if (error) {
 77 |         throw new Error(`Failed to update repository: ${error.message}`);
 78 |       }
 79 | 
 80 |       return data;
 81 |     } else {
 82 |       // Insert new repository
 83 |       const { data, error } = await supabase
 84 |         .from(TABLES.USER_REPOSITORIES)
 85 |         .insert({
 86 |           ...repository,
 87 |           last_synced_at: new Date().toISOString(),
 88 |         })
 89 |         .select()
 90 |         .single();
 91 | 
 92 |       if (error) {
 93 |         throw new Error(`Failed to save repository: ${error.message}`);
 94 |       }
 95 | 
 96 |       return data;
 97 |     }
 98 |   }
 99 | 
100 |   /**
101 |    * Delete a repository
102 |    */
103 |   async deleteRepository(repositoryId: string): Promise<void> {
104 |     const { error } = await supabase
105 |       .from(TABLES.USER_REPOSITORIES)
106 |       .delete()
107 |       .eq("id", repositoryId);
108 | 
109 |     if (error) {
110 |       throw new Error(`Failed to delete repository: ${error.message}`);
111 |     }
112 |   }
113 | 
114 |   /**
115 |    * Update repository sync timestamp
116 |    */
117 |   async updateSyncTimestamp(repositoryId: string): Promise<void> {
118 |     const { error } = await supabase
119 |       .from(TABLES.USER_REPOSITORIES)
120 |       .update({
121 |         last_synced_at: new Date().toISOString(),
122 |         updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
123 |       })
124 |       .eq("id", repositoryId);
125 | 
126 |     if (error) {
127 |       throw new Error(`Failed to update sync timestamp: ${error.message}`);
128 |     }
129 |   }
130 | }
131 | 
132 | /**
133 |  * Create a repository service instance
134 |  */
135 | export function createRepositoryService(): RepositoryService {
136 |   return new RepositoryService();
137 | }
138 | 
139 | // Made with Bob
